data.title: AI-Driven Unrestricted Financial Warfare: Alarms Raised by the Anthropic Mythos Model
data.description:
I. Event Overview: US Top Leaders Urgently Convene Meeting with Banking Giants to Address New AI Risks

In early April 2026, US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell urgently convened a meeting at the Treasury Department headquarters in Washington, D.C., with the CEOs of several systemically important banks to discuss the cybersecurity risks posed by Anthropic's latest AI model, Mythos (Claude Mythos Preview). This closed-door meeting was hastily arranged around April 7th. Participants included Citigroup CEO Jane Fraser, Morgan Stanley CEO Ted Pick, Bank of America CEO Brian Moynihan, Wells Fargo CEO Charlie Scharf, and Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon. JP Morgan CEO Jamie Dimon was unable to attend due to other commitments.

Technology Defense

Banks and infrastructure operators should invest in AI-assisted defense tools, implement continuous vulnerability scanning, sandbox isolation, and human oversight. Zero-trust architecture and quantum-secure encryption will be key focuses.

Enterprise and Individual Level

Diversify asset allocation and avoid over-reliance on a single digital system. Physical assets such as precious metals can serve as long-term hedging tools, but should be integrated into the overall portfolio.

International Coordination AI risks know no borders. Major economies such as the US, China, and the EU need to engage in dialogue on security standards to prevent arms race-style proliferation. In the long term, AI will reshape the financial and security landscape. The Mythos incident is an early warning sign, indicating that accelerated technological development may amplify vulnerabilities. Balancing innovation and security is a common global challenge. VII. Conclusion: Building Resilience Amidst the Double-Edged Sword of Technology The emergency meeting triggered by the Anthropic Mythos model highlights the disruptive potential of AI in cybersecurity. It is both a powerful defensive tool and a potential offensive weapon. For the global financial system, this serves as a reminder of the vulnerability of digital dependence. In an era of heightened uncertainty, gold and silver, as "non-intelligent" assets, offer a path back to fundamental value. Future development depends on the efforts of multiple parties: technology companies need to strengthen security and ethics, regulators need to update frameworks, and market participants need to enhance resilience. Precious metals will not solve all problems, but in their physical stability and historical role, they embody humanity's pursuit of lasting value. Through careful analysis and diversified preparation, societies can better navigate technological progress while mitigating systemic risks.

data.title: The SEC clarifies regulations for cryptocurrency trading interfaces; the next round of US-Iran talks may be held on the 16th.
data.description:
Headline

▌US SEC Statement Clarifies Regulatory Boundaries for Crypto Trading Interfaces: Brokerage Licenses Exempt in Specific Scenarios

The US Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) Division of Trading and Markets issued a staff statement providing guidance on whether certain covered user interfaces used to generate trading instructions for crypto asset securities need to be registered as brokers. The statement points out that, under certain conditions, providers of such interfaces may not need to register as broker-dealers under Section 15 of the Securities Exchange Act. These conditions include: not actively soliciting specific trades, not providing investment advice, not controlling or executing trades, generating trading instructions solely based on objective parameters, and fully disclosing to users the fee structure, potential conflicts of interest, and related risks. The SEC emphasizes that such interfaces typically exist in the form of websites, browser plugins, or wallet applications, used to convert user-defined trading parameters into on-chain executable instructions, while also providing market data such as prices, paths, and fees.

Such exemptions do not apply to activities such as participating in transaction matching, fund custody, order routing, or providing investment advice. These guidelines are interim opinions and will automatically expire five years later, starting April 13, 2026, unless further action is taken. This move aims to provide a clearer regulatory framework for activities related to crypto asset securities and to continuously solicit market feedback.

▌Next Round of US-Iran Talks May Be Held in Islamabad on the 16th

On April 14, according to a report by RIA Novosti citing the US magazine *The Atlantic*, the next round of "direct talks" between the United States and Iran may be held on the 16th in Islamabad, the capital of Pakistan.

(Xinhua News Agency) Market Data As of press time, according to CoinGecko data: BTC price is $74,758.68, a 24-hour change of +5.8%; ETH price is $2,376.19, a 24-hour change of +8.6%; BNB price is $616.52, a 24-hour change of +4.3%. SOL price is $86.50, a 24-hour change of +6.1%; DOGE price is $0.09414, a 24-hour change of +3.9%; XRP price is $1.38, a 24-hour change of +4.1%; TRX price is $0.321, a 24-hour change of... WLFI price is $0.08187, a 24-hour change of +4.6%; HYPE price is $44.28, a 24-hour change of +8.5%. Policy ▌White House advisor says crypto legislation disagreements are narrowing, progress is accelerating. On April 14, White House digital asset advisor Patrick Witt stated that previously considered "intractable" crypto legislation disagreements have significantly decreased, and the prospects for passing related legislation are improving. With Congress reconvening, a key Senate committee is expected to vote on a comprehensive crypto bill, paving the way for further legislation. This bill aims to clarify the regulatory roles of the SEC and CFTC and establish a unified regulatory framework for exchanges and market participants. Current controversy centers on stablecoin incentive mechanisms. While the passed stablecoin bill prohibits issuers from directly paying interest to users, disagreement remains regarding whether third-party platforms should be allowed to provide rewards. Overall, policy-making is shifting from "high uncertainty" to "gradual convergence," and the regulatory framework for the crypto industry is expected to enter a phase of substantial progress.

▌Coinbase CEO: The Time is Ripe for the US Clarity Act

April 13th news, according to Hodler's Digest, a subsidiary of Cointelegraph Magazine, Brian Armstrong, CEO of Coinbase, a leading US cryptocurrency exchange, publicly stated that after months of legislative delays and multi-party negotiations, the time is "ripe" for the passage of the US Clarity Act (the Digital Asset Markets Clarity Act). It is understood that Armstrong's statement represents a significant reversal of his stance on the bill. In January 2026, he twice publicly withdrew his support for the Clarity Act, citing flaws in its provisions, directly causing the Senate Banking Committee to postpone the bill's review and voting process, bringing the legislative process to a standstill.

... After months of bipartisan negotiations and extensive consultations between the crypto industry and the banking sector, Armstrong stated that the current version of the bill is a "powerful bill" and explicitly called on Congress to push for its enactment. The CLARITY Act, formally known as the Digital Asset Markets Clarity Act of 2025 (H.R. 3633), is the most comprehensive and core piece of cryptocurrency regulatory legislation ever enacted in the United States. Passed by a bipartisan majority in the U.S. House of Representatives on July 17, 2025, its core purpose is to clearly define the regulatory authority of the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) over digital assets, clarify the classification standards for "digital commodities" and "digital asset securities," end the long-standing regulatory ambiguity in the U.S. crypto market, and provide a framework for the industry's compliant development.

▌Vance Says Significant Progress Made in Iran Talks; Second Round of Negotiations Depends on Iran

On April 14, US Vice President Vance stated in an interview with Fox News that the US had made significant progress in negotiations with Iran. When asked if there would be more negotiations, Vance said the ball was in Tehran's court. He added that the US expects Iran to make progress on opening the Strait of Hormuz and warned that if Tehran does not do so, the situation in the negotiations will change.

▌Iranian Foreign Ministry: US "Constant Changes of Mind" Led to Failure to Reach Agreement

On April 13, the Iranian Foreign Ministry stated that the US's constant changes of demands during negotiations with Iran in Islamabad were the reason why the two sides failed to reach an agreement.

The Iranian Foreign Ministry said that Iranian Foreign Minister Araqchi stated in a phone call with French Foreign Minister Barro on the evening of April 12 that although Iran had "complete distrust" of the United States, it still participated in the negotiations in a responsible and goodwill manner and made progress on several issues. However, due to excessive demands and constant changes in the US's demands, an agreement was ultimately not reached. (Xinhua)

Blockchain Applications

▌Coinbase VP of International Policy Departs to Join OpenAI

On April 14, a Coinbase spokesperson stated via email that Duff Gordon, VP of International Policy who had worked at Coinbase for nearly four years, had left the exchange to join OpenAI as Head of EMEA Policy.

▌StarkWare to Layoffs and Reorganize into Two Business Units

On April 13, StarkWare announced layoffs and an organizational restructuring. StarkWare will form two business units: one is a revenue-oriented application business unit led by current CPO Avihu Levy, focusing on building revenue-generating products on its technology stack; the other is the Starknet development unit led by current Head of Product Tom Brand.

▌Strategy spent approximately $1 billion last week to purchase 13,927 Bitcoins

On April 13th, Strategy purchased 13,927 Bitcoins last week, totaling approximately $1 billion, at a price of approximately $71,902 per Bitcoin, achieving a 5.6% Bitcoin yield so far in 2026. As of April 12th, 2026, Strategy holds 780,897 Bitcoins, totaling approximately $59.02 billion, at a price of approximately $75,577 per Bitcoin.

...

▌BitMine increased its holdings by over 71,000 ETH last week, bringing its total holdings to over 4% of the total ETH supply

As of April 12th, Eastern Time, BitMine's total cryptocurrency + cash holdings + "Moonshot" holdings totaled $11.8 billion. BitMine held 4,874,858 ETH (an increase of 71,524 ETH from the previous week), representing 4.04% of the total ETH supply (120.7 million ETH). It also holds 197 BTC, $200 million worth of Beast Industries shares, $85 million worth of Eightco Holdings (NASDAQ: ORBS), and $719 million in uncollateralized cash. As of April 13th, 2026, BitMine's total staked ETH is 3,334,637 (equivalent to $7.4 billion at $2,206 per ETH).

▌MetaPlanet ADR ($MPJPY) Launches on Robinhood

According to market news: MetaPlanet's American Depositary Receipts (ADR, ticker symbol MPJPY) are now tradable on Robinhood; its depository bank, Deutsche Bank, announced that it will waive the ADR issuance fee until June 12, reducing the entry cost for US retail investors.

▌Polka Cross-Chain Bridge Attacked, 1 Billion DOT Tokens Maliciously Minted and Sold Off

On April 13, according to PeckShield monitoring, 1 billion DOT tokens were abnormally minted and sold off on the Ethereum chain.

On-chain data shows that about an hour ago, attackers tampered with administrator privileges, transferring control of the contract to a malicious address, then immediately issued 1 billion DOT tokens and sold them off, causing the price to plummet from $1.22 to near zero. The incident is still developing, and Polkadot has not yet issued a response. The target of this attack was a bridge asset on Ethereum, not the Polkadot native chain.

Important Economic Developments

▌US Military Begins Intercepting Ships Entering and Exiting the Strait of Hormuz

On the 13th local time, the US military began intercepting ships entering and exiting the Strait of Hormuz. The US Central Command said on the 12th that, according to the president's order, it would begin imposing a blockade on all maritime traffic entering and exiting Iranian ports at 10:00 AM Eastern Time on the 13th. The statement said that the blockade applies to all ships of all countries entering and exiting Iranian ports and the country's coastal areas, covering all Iranian ports in the Persian Gulf and the Gulf of Oman.

▌US Media: US Demands Iran Suspend Uranium Enrichment for 20 Years; Iran Hopes for Shorter Suspension Period

On April 14, according to AXIOS, a US official and a source familiar with the matter revealed that the US proposed a 20-year suspension of uranium enrichment for Iran during negotiations held in Islamabad over the weekend. Iran, however, proposed a shorter period, a single-digit number of years. Sources said the US demand for a suspension of uranium enrichment was a key issue in this marathon negotiation. "The US proposed at least a 20-year suspension, with various other restrictions attached," said a source familiar with the matter. The US also demanded that Iran remove all highly enriched uranium from the country. Iran, on the other hand, stated that they were willing to accept a "monitored de-enrichment process" for these materials.

...

▌Trump: Will Not Accept Any Agreement Allowing Iran to Possess Nuclear Weapons

On April 14, local time, US President Trump stated on Monday that Iran wants an agreement, but he will not accept any agreement that would allow Tehran to possess nuclear weapons. Trump said that negotiations surrounding the nuclear issue have reached a stalemate and indicated that a “blockade” against ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz has begun. He stated that Iran “called this morning” and said that “they want to reach an agreement.”

... "Iran will not have nuclear weapons," Trump told reporters at the White House. "We cannot allow one country to blackmail or extort the world." ▌Federal Reserve's Goolsby: Strong US Consumer Performance Key to the US Economy On April 14, Federal Reserve's Goolsby stated on Monday that current pricing in the oil futures market reflects an expectation that the surge in oil prices related to the war with Iran will be short-lived, and its impact on the US economy is likely to be limited as long as this holds true. In an interview with Fox News, Goolsby stated, "As long as consumers remain strong, I think economic growth will remain strong. If that continues—if gasoline prices remain at or above their current levels for an extended period—then we'll have to reassess what that means for consumer spending; but if things move as the market expects, then it will be temporary." Vance reveals details of negotiations: Impasse stems from insufficient Iranian authorization. On April 14th, according to Fox News, US Vice President Vance stated that the US delegation withdrew from negotiations after determining that Iranian negotiators lacked the authorization to reach a final agreement, adding that any progress now depends on approval from the Tehran leadership. He said, "I think we really understand some of the Iranians' negotiating style, and that's ultimately why we left Pakistan, because we found that, I think, the team there couldn't reach an agreement, and they had to go back to Tehran, either to get approval from the Supreme Leader or someone else, to accept the conditions we offered." Vance emphasized that Trump remains open to a more normalized relationship with Iran, provided key conditions are met. However, he stated that for Iran to become an economically normal country, it must become a normal country that does not pursue nuclear weapons, and it must also become a normal country that does not engage in "terrorism."

▌Vance: No Agreement Reached, But Progress is Smooth in Some Areas

On April 14, according to Fox News, US Vice President Vance stated that although no agreement was reached, progress was "very smooth" in some areas.

He said, "I wouldn't say things went completely wrong. I think there were good parts." He added, "(We clarified) under which terms the U.S. could compromise, on which terms we had flexibility, and what outcomes we absolutely needed to see in order for President Trump to feel he had gotten a good deal. In short, we had some good conversations. I think this is probably the first time the Iranian and U.S. governments have held such a high-level meeting since the current Iranian leadership came to power." According to CME's "FedWatch" on April 14th, the probability of the Fed raising interest rates by 25 basis points in April is 1%, and the probability of keeping rates unchanged is 99%. The probability of the Fed cumulatively cutting interest rates by 25 basis points by June is 1.5%, the probability of keeping rates unchanged is 97.5%, and the probability of cumulatively raising interest rates by 25 basis points is 1%.

Golden Encyclopedia

▌Can Bitcoin Still Be Called "Digital Gold" During Geopolitical Crises?

Every geopolitical crisis causes gold prices to rise while Bitcoin prices plummet. After six tests, the claim of "digital gold" has never been supported by data. Countries hoard gold but exclude Bitcoin from their reserves. For investors, Bitcoin exhibits asymmetry: it falls with stocks but does not rise with them. Three structural asymmetries prevent Bitcoin from achieving safe-haven status: an oversupply of derivatives (market structure), the dominance of leveraged traders (participant composition), and a lack of repeatable behavioral records (behavioral accumulation).

Bitcoin is not a safe-haven asset, but it is a "crisis-useful asset," which can indeed function when borders are closed and banks fail. If these three asymmetries are reduced, Bitcoin may no longer be a replica of gold but rather a completely new "next-generation gold."

Generational shifts and the widespread application of algorithms are key factors that could accelerate this process.
